persona.the_one_thing: Yeop Lee's literal job is recruiting startups onto Claude across APAC — so walk in as a founder seeking a program fit, NOT a buyer cold-pitching a vendor; his incentive is to say yes to good builders and platform them. (High confidence — based on his title + a year of founder-program activity, s7/s29/s8.)
persona.snapshot: Yeop Lee is Head of Startup Partnerships, APAC at Anthropic, based in Seoul (s1, s7). His role is to grow Claude's founder/developer ecosystem across the region — he runs and hosts founder salons, build days, hackathons and accelerators in Seoul, Melbourne, Singapore, Tokyo and Bangalore (s8, s9, s23, s29, s10). He is highly public-facing: a NextRise 2026 keynote speaker and a frequent panel moderator (s12, s5).
persona.whats_new: Over the last ~12 months his footprint widened from Korea-centric to APAC-wide: Korea AI-hub commitment (Jun 2025, s25), Seoul Founder House sold out (Apr 2026, s8), Melbourne/Australia salons (Feb–Apr 2026, s23, s24), a 'push to prod' hackathon in Singapore (Mar 2026, s29), India programs incl. an Anthropic×AWS Agentic AI Accelerator and a Bangalore CTO event (s11, s10), and a Consumer AI Breakout (s28). He is now running a full multi-country founder-recruitment engine, not a single-market role.

top_moves.fact: Head of Startup Partnerships, APAC; personally runs founder salons, build days and hackathons (s7, s9, s29).
top_moves.inference: Orbt's ask (a startup partnership / Claude adoption) is the exact thing he's incentivized to enable — he is not a gatekeeper to be sold, he's a funnel-owner looking for good builders.
top_moves.move: Open by asking how orbt plugs into his APAC founder programs (credits + visibility + accelerator), not 'can we use Claude' — let him slot you into something he already runs.

top_moves.inference: Orbt's cross-border F&B/cosmetics consumer angle sits squarely in a theme he actively programs — it's a clean thematic match, not a stretch.
top_moves.move: Lead with the consumer-AI agent angle for Korean F&B/beauty brands going cross-border; frame orbt as a showcase consumer-AI story for his pipeline.

top_moves.fact: Publicly amplified Anthropic's commitment to the Korean market (s25) and runs Seoul Founder House / Build Day (s8, s9).
top_moves.inference: A Korean cross-border startup story directly advances the Korea-hub narrative he's been selling internally and publicly.
top_moves.move: Tie orbt's cross-border expansion to his Korea-hub thesis — hand him a Korean success story he can platform to the APAC ecosystem.

non_obvious.insight: His public footprint is not a sales pipeline — it's a founder-recruitment-and-amplification engine: Founder House (sold out), hackathons, build days, multi-country accelerators, keynotes platforming founders. A partnership with him plausibly comes with distribution/visibility (being featured to the APAC ecosystem), not just API credits — so ask for the stage, not only the discount.

chemistry.overall_read: A genuinely complementary pairing on paper: Sunwoo is exactly the kind of technical, ship-focused Seoul AI founder that Yeop's whole APAC role exists to find and platform, and they share Korean-AI-ecosystem and consumer/agentic-AI ground. Confidence is capped low because public data on Yeop is thin (coverage 0.4) — this is a directional read, not a verdict.

mbti_compat.read: Architect-builder meets connector-host: the introverted-rigor type supplies substance, the warm extraverted type supplies reach and relationships — classic mutual-fill, provided the builder doesn't read warmth as fluff.
mbti_compat.label: directional

shared_interests.point: Korean AI / startup ecosystem as a deliberate focus
shared_interests.me_evidence: Lists 'The Korean startup ecosystem' as a named interest; orbt is a Seoul-based AI-native consulting platform
shared_interests.them_evidence: Champions 'Korea as an AI innovation hub for Anthropic' and runs Seoul founder programs (Founder House, AI-hub commitment)

shared_interests.me_evidence: Builds AI agents for Korean F&B and cosmetics brands (consumer goods) and an 'intelligence engine' for D2C
shared_interests.them_evidence: Co-hosts a Consumer AI Breakout; names consumer-AI applications as a focus

shared_interests.me_evidence: Lists 'AI-native / agentic systems' first among what he cares about and builds orbt's agents
shared_interests.them_evidence: Runs an Anthropic×AWS Agentic AI Accelerator and Claude founder/developer programs

similar_traits.me_signal: 'I ship… I want the reasoning to hold up'; frames self as Olympiad Medalist + CTO, proof over adjectives
similar_traits.them_signal: Orchestrates recurring, scheduled, multi-city structured programs (Founder House, hackathons, accelerators)

complementary_traits.me_signal: INTJ, heads-down technical engine, judges on rigor and what he builds, lean/fast
complementary_traits.them_signal: Highly public-facing keynote speaker and salon/panel moderator who recruits and platforms founders
complementary_traits.why_it_helps: Yeop can give Sunwoo's quietly-built substance distribution, ecosystem access and Claude tooling; Sunwoo gives Yeop a credible, shipping technical founder to platform — each covers the other's blind spot.

complementary_traits.trait: Selective/loyalty-weighted trust vs. broadly relational generosity
complementary_traits.me_signal: 'I trust durable partnerships over flashy new ideas'; ~3-year pre-orbt team
complementary_traits.them_signal: Publicly thanks and credits others, platforms founders over himself — relationship-first style
complementary_traits.why_it_helps: Yeop's genuine, non-self-promoting crediting style is well-suited to earning a guarded, proof-first founder's trust over repeated, substantive contact rather than one pitch.

potential_friction.point: Proof-first skepticism toward a highly promotional, public-facing operator
potential_friction.me_signal: 'Proof over adjectives'; wary of 'flashy new ideas'; wants the reasoning to hold up
potential_friction.them_signal: Keynotes, salons, hackathons, broad ecosystem-enthusiasm framing
potential_friction.how_to_navigate: Yeop should open with something concrete and technical — a specific Claude capability, a relevant agentic build, or a precise founder/partner intro — rather than general ecosystem energy, or Sunwoo may discount it as surface.

potential_friction.me_signal: Values 'durable partnerships' and judges relationships by depth
potential_friction.how_to_navigate: Signal genuine long-horizon interest in orbt specifically (its cross-border thesis), not just funnel membership; a single concrete follow-through beats broad program invitations.

interaction_simulation.opening_30s: Likely warm and easy from Yeop's side — he opens relationally, probably referencing orbt's Meet the Drapers / cross-border work; Sunwoo answers crisply and a bit reserved, scanning for substance.
interaction_simulation.likely_arc: Warms quickly once the conversation moves to agentic architecture, Claude tooling, and the Korean consumer-AI/cross-border angle — common ground both can speak to concretely; Yeop spots a platformable founder, Sunwoo spots distribution and tooling.
interaction_simulation.where_it_could_stall: If Yeop stays in broad ecosystem/enthusiasm mode too long, Sunwoo's proof-first filter reads it as fluff and he goes terse; or if the pitch feels like generic funnel recruitment.
interaction_simulation.how_to_adapt: Drop into a specific technical or go-to-market problem within the first few minutes and offer one concrete, asymmetric value (a build-day slot, a Claude credit/capability fit, a named intro) — that converts Sunwoo's skepticism into engagement.

honest_gaps: chemistry is a low-confidence inference from public text, not a relationship prediction
coverage on Yeop is thin (0.4) and his MBTI/personality is largely inferred from observed event-running behavior, so trait reads are directional only
shared interests reflect overlapping public focus areas, not evidence the two have ever interacted
